215620.explanation: Our vibrant culture has put Heerlen on the map. Culture makes our city lively and brings people together. We must cherish this. We find it important to keep investing so everyone can participate and enjoy.

215621.explanation: Real grass is more comfortable for many athletes. With proper maintenance, we ensure healthy and green sports fields.

215622.explanation: Sports brings people together and is good for health. By investing in sports in all neighborhoods, we give everyone the chance to participate.

215623.explanation: Free fitness equipment makes exercising easy and fun. This allows people to work on their health close to home.

215624.explanation: Everyone must be able to be themselves at work. We believe enforcers should perform their duties professionally through their behavior, not through what they wear.

215625.explanation: Funding for care must reach the people who need it. By tackling healthcare fraud, more money remains for good care.

215626.explanation: Public use of drugs and alcohol can cause nuisance. With clear rules and enforcement, we ensure a safe and pleasant public space.

215627.explanation: We want to build strong and stable communities throughout the city. That includes a good mix of types of housing. With such a mix, we rebuild mixed neighborhoods where the children of surgeons and janitors play together in the street.

215628.explanation: By also building social rental homes in more expensive neighborhoods (and vice versa!), we create more balance throughout the city.

215629.explanation: By managing the housing market well, the municipality ensures that homes are built that are truly needed in Heerlen. With clear rules, we can tackle profiteering landlords and build what suits starters, families, and seniors.

215630.explanation: It is important to handle limited space and facilities carefully. For caravan sites, we consider what fits the city and surroundings. Since Heerlen already has the most caravan sites in the region, it is good to first build more in other locations.

215631.explanation: We want to assist with necessary veterinary care for people who truly cannot afford it. That help must be focused and with clear limits, because paying for everything would be unfair to other residents.

215632.explanation: People on welfare often face stress and stacked problems. We believe more in trust and good guidance rather than harsh penalties. This way, we better help people towards work. If that does not succeed for a while, volunteering can be a good start.

215633.explanation: Help from family or friends can provide support in difficult times. We think it is unfair to punish people for this with a lower benefit.

215634.explanation: Work must always pay off. But if people earn a little extra, they should really be allowed to keep something without immediate deductions. This helps people get going and enables them to leave welfare when they earn a full income.

215635.explanation: Many elderly want and can still work. With additional projects, we help them find suitable work and utilize their experience and talents.

215636.explanation: Speed bumps are often necessary to keep streets safe, especially for children and cyclists. Fewer speed bumps can lead to faster driving and more accidents. Traffic safety must always be a priority.

215637.explanation: Parking fees can help make the city more beautiful and livable. We want this money to also be used for safety, greenery, and local amenities.

215638.explanation: Car sharing makes it easier to use a car occasionally. With more car-sharing spots, we save space and reduce congestion.

215639.explanation: Calm traffic makes residential neighborhoods safer. This is nice for children, seniors, and everyone who cycles or walks.

215641.explanation: Strong shoulders can bear more burden. Municipal assistance is for those who need it. If someone can afford a walker themselves, the municipality can use that money to help those who cannot.

215642.explanation: Care by family and neighborhood is valuable but must remain voluntary and supplementary. The municipality must provide good professional care and not overburden people.

215643.explanation: Care community centers help the elderly live independently and meet each other. This provides conviviality, support, and a strong neighborhood.

215644.explanation: All children deserve good support. Help with learning or dyslexia must remain accessible to everyone, regardless of parents' income.

215645.explanation: Projects where young and old help each other create contact and understanding. This makes neighborhoods warmer and stronger.

215585.explanation: PHB believes taxes should remain as low as possible but notes this must be done responsibly. Our city still needs to be maintained-think of roads, sewers, and greenery.

215586.explanation: PHB has wanted the dog tax abolished for years. The proceeds of this tax are not specifically used. We have achieved a lower tax for the first dog but want it completely abolished.

215587.explanation: PHB wants to support resident initiatives. Joint energy production falls under this. However, the financial support is not unlimited. We also aim for a financially healthy municipality.

215588.explanation: Asylum seekers who are not allowed to stay in the Netherlands must return to their own country.

215589.explanation: PHB finds this unfair to our own residents. Waiting lists apply to everyone, so there should be no exceptions for refugees with residence permits.

215590.explanation: PHB believes we currently fund enough instruments for the integration of newcomers into society. We want the effectiveness of these instruments to be examined and improved if necessary.

215591.explanation: PHB is concerned about high energy costs and understands people have chosen alternative energy sources such as wood stoves in recent years.

215592.explanation: PHB sees that green maintenance has improved over recent years and believes this trend must continue, but mowing should be done more with common sense.

215594.explanation: PHB has supported all motions regarding the expansion of municipal enforcement officers. This is because police capacity is insufficient and we believe that the safety perception of our residents is important. Goal: more blue on the streets!

215595.explanation: PHB would be a strong supporter of this. This way, the community police officer becomes a familiar face and better understands what is happening in the neighborhood. We believe that services, including police services, should be close to the residents.

215596.explanation: PHB acknowledges that a BOA (municipal enforcement officer) is not a police officer. So we must be careful about which tasks BOAs may or may not perform. However, as we get fewer police officers, BOAs will have to fill the gap. This implies that the basic requirements for this profession will have to be raised.

215597.explanation: PHB finds it unacceptable that buildings stand empty while our residents need housing.

215599.explanation: PHB believes that everyone has the right to make ends meet financially and is positive about a basic income because trials in Finland have shown that it has a positive effect. However, this is not a municipal matter but should be implemented nationally.

215600.explanation: PHB does not want to burden entrepreneurs with extra costs. We see that there are more and more electric cars and transport vehicles, which is a good thing, but let people and entrepreneurs decide when they purchase these themselves. Do not impose it.

215601.explanation: PHB believes that entrepreneurs already have a hard time and that businesses must remain easily accessible.

215602.explanation: PHB finds the current policy sufficient and believes this limits parking space for regular cars.

215603.explanation: PHB finds it most important that our children receive the help they need. Price is important, but quality is much more important.

215604.explanation: PHB believes poverty must be addressed. This needs to be tackled more broadly. We find this statement too limited because simply distributing sanitary pads without specifying for whom is, in our view, an oversimplification.

215605.explanation: PHB supports improving municipal services and believes an independent ombudsman could improve quality.

215606.explanation: PHB finds it more important that letters are readable. So let’s focus on that.

215607.explanation: PHB believes there should be fewer rules for entrepreneurs. A small terrace should not be a problem, but must be reported. The condition is that residents are not restricted in their use of public space (users of walkers or wheelchairs should have enough space).

215608.explanation: PHB wants entrepreneurs to be facilitated to do business. If we support residents in sustainability, this should also apply to entrepreneurs.

215609.explanation: PHB believes the municipality should not always give money; such initiatives can be facilitated.

215611.explanation: PHB believes we must treat our labor migrants properly, who are often here to do work that is sorely needed and otherwise would not be done. If things go wrong, they should be helped to return to their country.

215613.explanation: PHB finds the fine high enough, but there must be more enforcement and fines issued. In addition, other solutions must be found to prevent illegal dumping.

215614.explanation: PHB has been a supporter of the national program Heerlen-North from the start, which is bearing fruit. We believe this program must be unconditionally continued and supported.

215615.explanation: PHB finds this is still insufficiently developed to simply invest money in. We wonder if owners of solar panels and neighbors cannot arrange this in a cooperative (after all, they also benefit), and the municipality could play a facilitating role.

215616.explanation: PHB believes residents and local entrepreneurs should make decisions about trees on their own property. However, if a tree is in public space, a permit is required.

215617.explanation: PHB believes animals must always be treated well. We consider the use of animals at events for educational purposes acceptable because this allows our children to get to know animals.

215620.explanation: PHB finds this a very general statement. If we allocate less money here, what will we not do and what will we still do? Therefore, a neutral answer.

215621.explanation: PHB believes associations should decide this themselves.

215622.explanation: PHB believes that sports should be accessible to everyone nearby because it contributes to health.

215623.explanation: PHB believes that sports should be accessible to everyone because it contributes to health.

215624.explanation: PHB believes religious expressions do not belong on uniforms.

215625.explanation: PHB believes it is important that everyone receives the care they need. We think quality should be reviewed to see if it is going well and if fraud is detected: tackle it. But do not spend more money on fraud detection without cause.

215626.explanation: PHB believes the nuisance this causes must be addressed, so fines should be imposed as this is also a tool to tackle the underlying problems.

215627.explanation: PHB supports a good mix in every neighborhood. So a mix of rental and owner-occupied homes in all segments makes neighborhoods stronger.

215628.explanation: PHB advocates for better distribution of social rental homes and expensive owner-occupied homes.

215629.explanation: PHB believes the municipality should set rules, mainly to solve skewed balances in certain neighborhoods.

215630.explanation: PHB basically believes there are enough places.

215631.explanation: PHB finds animal welfare important. So if someone has a pet and no money to pay the vet, this may be covered in our view, but it should not be the case that people acquire unlimited animals and the costs fall on the community.

215632.explanation: PHB believes everyone should participate in society according to their abilities. So if you receive social assistance benefits, you are expected to actively look for work or volunteer. Benefits should be reduced with no participation.

215633.explanation: PHB believes family or friends should be able to help people on social assistance. We would like that threshold to be higher.

215634.explanation: PHB believes that work must pay off. People should be guided out of benefits into a job as quickly as possible; if benefits remain alongside earnings, we remove the incentive. The exception is reimbursement for volunteer work or informal care.

215635.explanation: PHB believes the municipality already invests a lot in labor market policy. Let's first look at other unemployed people before focusing only on the elderly.

215636.explanation: PHB believes far too many speed bumps have been installed and this is not always successful. More creative measures, developed in consultation with residents, should be considered.

215637.explanation: PHB believes that if money is collected from a target group, that money should also be spent on that group.

215638.explanation: PHB wants to keep the limited parking space we already have as free as possible and not restrict it through various prior regulations.

215639.explanation: PHB considers this a good rule because it makes it clear to everyone that quiet driving is required in every residential neighborhood. This ensures more safety in our residential areas.

215641.explanation: PHB believes that assistance should be provided based on the individual's ability to pay. So if someone has sufficient means themselves, they can pay, allowing us to help those who cannot afford it.

215642.explanation: PHB believes we should move towards caring neighborhoods, but it should not be that the municipality requires mandatory help from family, friends, or the community. The municipality must remain responsible and facilitate and encourage such assistance.

215643.explanation: PHB finds it important that elderly people can continue living in their own environment and find suitable activities so they do not become lonely. Therefore, we definitely support more care community centers.

215644.explanation: PHB believes every child should receive the help they need to develop optimally, regardless of their parents’ financial situation.

215645.explanation: PHB wants connections between residents in every neighborhood so that people can help each other in so-called caring neighborhoods. We therefore support such projects.

215585.explanation: The municipality itself must make sharper choices so that taxes can be lowered.

215586.explanation: This indirect tax is no longer appropriate these days.

215587.explanation: This is not a task of the municipality.

215588.explanation: If the law says you are not allowed to be here, we should not accommodate them. This goes against national policy.

215589.explanation: That is a form of inequality of opportunity. Residents of Heerlen also have the right to social rental housing.

215590.explanation: It is always important-and the responsibility-of newcomers to integrate. Projects can support this, but it is not necessarily the municipality’s duty.

215591.explanation: It is not wrong for the municipality to hold residents accountable for their responsibility and refer them to the RIVM’s Heating Guide.

215592.explanation: Public green spaces must be maintained properly, but that does not mean mowing more frequently.

215594.explanation: If you set rules about safety, you must enforce them. Enforcement officers are also important for the feeling of safety.

215595.explanation: That does not have to be structural for every neighborhood. The neighborhood police officer must be known and easily accessible.

215596.explanation: That can be done if the safety situation requires it. The starting point is national policy.

215597.explanation: The initiator must also arrange financing. The municipality can facilitate, for example, by adjusting zoning plans.

215599.explanation: If the government requests Heerlen as a pilot area and finances it, that is possible. Not on our own initiative.

215600.explanation: This has a negligible effect. Such a measure in Heerlen misses the mark.

215601.explanation: Absolutely. Creating barriers does not promote the livability of Heerlen’s city center.

215602.explanation: If it has been demonstrated that there is a shortage.

215603.explanation: It is about the combination of quality and a good price.

215604.explanation: It is not a task of the municipality.

215605.explanation: There are sufficient options for residents to make complaints known to the municipality. Note: the municipality is affiliated with the National Ombudsman.

215606.explanation: Society is changing, and inclusive behavior from the municipality is part of that.

215607.explanation: That can be done as long as safety and accessibility are not compromised.

215608.explanation: It is up to the entrepreneur, in terms of their own responsibility, if they want to make things more sustainable. The municipality should facilitate this.

215609.explanation: Encouragement is a good thing, but subsidizing it is not a municipal task.

215611.explanation: That is not a municipal task. However, the municipality is allowed to hold companies accountable to take responsibility in this area.

215613.explanation: As long as it is enforced.

215614.explanation: If the various programs prove successful, that can be done. An important condition is that a careful consideration must be made whether the project has real chances. Note: a contribution can act as a flywheel for further success.

215615.explanation: This is not a municipal task.

215616.explanation: Except for trees classified as monumental.

215617.explanation: This is no longer appropriate these days.

215620.explanation: In principle, cultural organizations should be self-supporting.

215621.explanation: Artificial turf is a good-and cheaper-alternative to grass, with the added benefit that it can be used more intensively by clubs.

215622.explanation: Sport is an individual responsibility of people.

215623.explanation: Sport is an individual responsibility of people. Sport does not have to be linked to equipment. The municipality encourages sport in general.

215624.explanation: This is not the municipality’s responsibility. As an authority figure, you must convey uniformity.

215625.explanation: Fraud is always wrong. In cases of fraud, even healthcare fraud, you must always demand your money back.

215626.explanation: These are activities that do not belong in public spaces. Activities causing nuisance must always be addressed.

215627.explanation: A balanced residential composition is beneficial for social cohesion in a neighborhood.

215628.explanation: The municipality can steer, but it is about finding a good balance. Steering too hard can have the effect of no building happening at all.

215629.explanation: The municipality may, in certain cases, steer the typology (type) of homes, but this should not be rigidly fixed in advance.

215630.explanation: Heerlen already exceeds the norm for the number of pitches for caravan dwellers significantly.

215631.explanation: In principle, it is the individual's own responsibility. In very specific cases, a contribution can be granted through special social assistance.

215632.explanation: Social assistance is primarily a temporary measure to help you. It is not a permanent benefit.

215633.explanation: Social assistance is primarily a temporary measure to help you out of a difficult situation.

215634.explanation: Social assistance is primarily a temporary measure to help you out of a difficult situation.

215635.explanation: Generally, it is harder for elderly people to find a new job. Promoting employment projects for seniors can help with this.

215636.explanation: There are sufficient other traffic-restricting measures available to achieve the same effect.

215637.explanation: It is up to the municipality to allocate tax revenue towards other matters.

215638.explanation: The number of people (support base) in Heerlen using a shared car is insufficient to create additional parking spaces.

215639.explanation: Generally, this is already the case in neighborhoods in Heerlen. Lowering speed should enhance safety, and enforcement is an important aspect. However, sometimes main roads run through neighborhoods, where lowering speed may be undesirable.

215641.explanation: Purchasing an aid device is primarily the responsibility of the individual.

215642.explanation: The municipality already does this partially.

215643.explanation: It is a worthy goal to facilitate a multifunctional 'old' seniors' home. This promotes neighborhood cohesion and can enhance housing market flow.

215644.explanation: This is the individual's own responsibility and also a task for education, not the municipality.

215645.explanation: These kinds of initiatives are not the responsibility of a municipality.

215614.explanation: It must come from The Hague.

215616.explanation: A permit is required for large trees, but not for smaller ones.

215620.explanation: Funding for festivals and cultural events that promote local culture, traditions, and regional language. No funding for elitist art or multicultural events.

215626.explanation: Yes, in the case of drug use.

215634.explanation: Depends on the extent of usage.

215636.explanation: But at the correct height; there are several thresholds that are too high according to new guidelines.

215637.explanation: Only for roads and bike paths, because they can be much improved.

215639.explanation: But where it really becomes dangerous, the speed must be reduced to 30 km/h.

215585.explanation: Affordability is important, but the municipality must also be able to invest in housing, education, healthcare, and sustainability. D66 Heerlen chooses a balanced approach where burdens are fairly distributed and vulnerable groups are protected.

215586.explanation: The current dog tax is not targeted and does not contribute to a cleaner public space. D66 chooses for partial abolition of the dog tax. The revenue from the remaining tax must be spent on dog-walking facilities and a cleaner living environment.

215587.explanation: Local energy initiatives accelerate the energy transition and create support. By providing assistance, the municipality helps residents invest together in affordable, clean energy.

215588.explanation: D66 Heerlen stands for human dignity, but believes that structural shelter for rejected asylum seekers is a national responsibility. The municipality must ensure humane emergency shelter and guidance where necessary.

215589.explanation: The housing shortage calls for more affordable homes, not for pitting groups against each other. D66 Heerlen advocates for fair housing policies and acceleration of housing construction.

215590.explanation: Good integration promotes self-reliance, participation, and social cohesion. Investing in language, work, and meeting spaces yields long-term benefits for the entire city.

215591.explanation: Wood burning negatively affects air quality and health. D66 Heerlen believes the municipality should actively promote cleaner alternatives. However, enforceability remains a challenge.

215592.explanation: D66 Heerlen seeks a balance between biodiversity and livability. Less mowing contributes to more biodiversity, but safety and accessibility must be guaranteed. Edges should be neatly mowed, as well as playgrounds for youth.

215594.explanation: More visible enforcement contributes to safety, especially when combined with prevention and neighborhood-focused work.

215595.explanation: D66 Heerlen finds it important that there is easy access to the community police officer. This strengthens trust in the police and such consultation hours can prevent escalation of problems.

215596.explanation: Searching (frisking) is an invasive authority that belongs to the police and requires strict safeguards.

215597.explanation: D66 Heerlen sees the importance of transforming office buildings to reduce the housing shortage and prevent vacancy. National regulations must particularly be relaxed to make this possible. Subsidy funds should be spent on verifiable improvements.

215599.explanation: D66 Heerlen views a basic income pilot as an opportunity to investigate whether trust and simplicity give people more room to find work, learn and participate. Experimenting helps base policy on facts instead of assumptions.

215600.explanation: Clean air and health are priorities for D66 Heerlen; an environmental zone can contribute to this.

215601.explanation: D66 Heerlen chooses a balance between accessibility and livability, with a priority on sustainable mobility.

215602.explanation: Good charging infrastructure is necessary for the energy transition and encourages residents and entrepreneurs to switch to sustainable transport.

215603.explanation: Quality and continuity of care are crucial. D66 Heerlen wants all children and families to receive appropriate support, with financial aspects considered but of lesser importance.

215604.explanation: This contributes to equal opportunities and prevents period poverty from limiting participation in school and work.

215605.explanation: An independent ombudsman increases transparency, strengthens trust, and makes handling complaints easily accessible.

215606.explanation: Inclusive communication ensures that all residents feel acknowledged and suits a modern, diverse city. Additionally, D66 Heerlen finds it very important that municipal letters are understandable and readable for everyone.

215607.explanation: D66 Heerlen sees space for entrepreneurs but wants this to happen within frameworks that prevent nuisance to the environment.

215608.explanation: Subsidies for the sustainability improvements of local entrepreneurs help the local economy and climate goals. The national government has already set up various subsidy schemes, such as the Sustainable Energy Production and Climate Transition scheme. The municipality can connect where there is need.

215609.explanation: Repair cafés promote a circular economy, social encounters, and knowledge sharing.

215611.explanation: Humane guidance prevents homelessness and exploitation and aligns with D66’s principle of human scale and solidarity.

215613.explanation: D66 Heerlen wants a clean city but believes enforcement alone is not enough. Prevention, awareness, and providing sufficient waste facilities are equally important to effectively combat littering.

215614.explanation: Long-term and targeted investments are necessary to effectively tackle social disadvantages, poverty, and inequality of opportunity in Heerlen-North. D66 Heerlen focuses on sustainable results and resident involvement.

215615.explanation: Neighborhood batteries increase self-sufficiency and energy efficiency. They strengthen local initiatives and the energy transition, fit sustainable urban development, and encourage cooperation among residents.

215616.explanation: Trees are crucial for climate adaptation, biodiversity, and livability. Preserving and carefully managing green spaces requires municipal frameworks and considerations.

215617.explanation: Animal welfare is important, so it is good to consider the safety and well-being of animals at events. D66 Heerlen does not consider any use of animals at events (such as no horse for Sinterklaas) excessive but wants to restrict the use of exotic animals.

215620.explanation: D66 Heerlen finds culture essential for identity, social connection, and participation. Investing in culture supports livability and inclusivity.

215621.explanation: The choice between artificial and natural grass should be based on usage, sustainability, and environmental impact, with attention to costs and playability. D66 Heerlen appreciates that there are many artificial turf fields allowing youth to play sports. However, safety considerations must always be made.

215622.explanation: Sports contribute to health, social encounters, and equal opportunities. D66 Heerlen wants widely accessible sports facilities in all neighborhoods.

215623.explanation: Free, low-threshold fitness facilities contribute to health, social encounters, and social cohesion.

215624.explanation: Freedom of religion and personal expression are core values. The Council of State gave a negative advice on a motion in the House of Representatives and the subsequent General Administrative Order on this matter, as it affects multiple fundamental rights such as the right to equal treatment and freedom of religion.

215625.explanation: Fraud harms people who need care and undermines trust in public services. D66 Heerlen wants effective detection with respect for legal protection.

215626.explanation: Public nuisance and safety require enforcement combined with prevention and care.

215627.explanation: D66 Heerlen believes mixed neighborhoods with rental and owner-occupied housing create livable communities, more opportunities, and less segregation. By smartly using demolition and new construction, a balanced neighborhood arises where people with different incomes live together comfortably.

215628.explanation: This promotes diversity and ensures everyone has access to good living environments. Additionally, we must bring more balance to all neighborhoods and especially bring the housing market in Heerlen more into balance.

215629.explanation: D66 Heerlen wants control over housing development to ensure affordability, diversity, and livability.

215630.explanation: Although caravan dwellers have the right to adequate and good housing and respect for their living culture, Heerlen already has relatively many places for caravan residents. According to D66 Heerlen, other municipalities should first restore balance.

215631.explanation: D66 Heerlen believes having a pet is a personal responsibility, including medical costs. Municipal funds should primarily go to core tasks such as poverty alleviation and care for people. Cooperation with animal welfare organizations is more appropriate here.

215632.explanation: D66 Heerlen believes social assistance is a safety net, not a final destination. Actively seeking work is part of this. Reductions if obligations are not met are fair to workers and stimulate labor market participation.

215633.explanation: Help from family or friends should not be penalized; it fosters social cohesion and solidarity. However, it is important to set a maximum amount.

215634.explanation: Earning extra income should pay off; this encourages participation and independence. According to D66 Heerlen, setting a maximum amount and limiting the duration is important.

215635.explanation: Seniors have experience and knowledge that the labor market can utilize. D66 Heerlen wants to actively support these talents.

215636.explanation: D66 Heerlen believes the number of speed bumps should always be proportional to the safety created. Currently, many speed bumps are installed in Heerlen, especially in Heerlen-North. According to D66, this is disproportionate.

215637.explanation: D66 Heerlen wants flexible use of resources for broader social goals such as accessibility, livability, and sustainability.

215638.explanation: Encourages sustainable mobility, reduces car ownership, and contributes to a livable city.

215639.explanation: Increases traffic safety, especially for children and the elderly, and contributes to livability.

215641.explanation: Support is targeted to those who need it.

215642.explanation: Informal care is valuable but cannot replace professional care and should not be mandatory.

215643.explanation: Care community centers combine independent living with social interaction and support close to home.

215644.explanation: The municipality supports guidance through neighborhood centers and schools where possible. Only those who cannot bear the burdens are helped, since prevention and equal opportunities for everyone are essential in Heerlen.

215645.explanation: D66 Heerlen believes this connects generations, promotes social cohesion, and encourages active citizenship.

215585.explanation: The municipality must take responsibility for a fair and sustainable city and needs public funds for this. Moreover, lowering property tax causes inequality between homeowners and tenants. We find that unacceptable.

215586.explanation: For years we have advocated for the abolition of the dog tax because it does not contribute to animal welfare and mainly affects people with a smaller budget. As long as the dog tax has not been abolished, the proceeds will go exclusively to animal welfare.

215587.explanation: By financially supporting residents' initiatives for sustainable energy, we ensure fair sustainability and local ownership. This contributes to energy justice. The municipality must ensure that the necessary conditions are in place.

215588.explanation: The municipality must provide humane shelter, including to people without residence status, for humanitarian reasons. Every person has the right to at least a bed, bath, and bread.

215589.explanation: Any housing shortage will be addressed with more affordable homes, considering Heerlen’s priority system. Not by playing people against each other. Also, from a humanitarian perspective, refugees, who often have traumatic backgrounds, have the right to a safe home.

215590.explanation: Good integration is an investment in an inclusive and healthy society. By investing in language, participation, and guidance, we ensure newcomers can fully participate and strengthen social cohesion in the neighborhood.

215591.explanation: The use of open fireplaces and wood stoves causes air pollution, health risks, and nuisance to others. Therefore, we advocate for a ban on wood burning and a subsidy scheme for the removal of wood stoves.

215592.explanation: What looks untidy to people is vital for insects, birds, and soil life. Mowing less is therefore better. In addition, we want grass fields to be enriched with native organic flowers and herbs to truly boost biodiversity.

215594.explanation: More enforcers are not a structural solution if the underlying causes of insecurity are not addressed. Safety is more than enforcement; it also concerns care, youth work, and prevention.

215595.explanation: The community police officer must be present more often and clearly visible. Accessible community officers can contribute to prevention and trust in the neighborhood. Low-threshold contact helps to identify problems early.

215596.explanation: Pat-down searches are an invasive power suitable only for police and justice, not for municipal enforcers. Intensive training is required due to the risk of ethnic profiling or abuse of power. The deployment of street coaches is more effective.

215597.explanation: By converting vacant offices into homes, we better utilize existing space, prevent building on green areas, and contribute to sustainable living. The building must be suitable for extensive sustainability improvements. Conversion prevents deterioration and promotes livability.

215599.explanation: A basic income trial can contribute to less poverty, less stress, and more well-being. By giving people trust and security, we invest in a city where the gap between rich and poor becomes smaller. We learn from pilots in other cities.

215600.explanation: An environmental zone for older diesel cars and trucks helps reduce air pollution downtown and contributes to a healthy and livable city. Additionally, we must stimulate cycling and public transport, plant lots of greenery, and limit wood burning.

215601.explanation: Accessibility does not have to mean that the car is central. The municipality should opt for healthy and sustainable mobility, free public transport, with tailored solutions for those dependent on cars, such as people with disabilities and care traffic.

215602.explanation: More charging points, also for people without a private driveway, are needed to enable the transition to cleaner mobility and reduce air pollution. Electric shared cars and other sustainable and affordable initiatives for shared transport and clean public transport are encouraged.

215603.explanation: In youth care, quality and continuity must be leading. Price should never be more important than good and appropriate care, emphasizing short waiting lists to prevent problems from worsening. The youth are our future and must be invested in.

215604.explanation: Menstrual products are a basic necessity. No one should be without these products due to lack of money. Free availability prevents inequality and promotes health and dignity. We advocate for more distribution points for menstrual products (MUP's) in collaboration with, among others, the Poverty Fund.

215605.explanation: An independent ombudsman ensures easy complaint handling and a transparent government.

215606.explanation: Gender-neutral communication ensures all residents feel addressed and respected by the municipality. It can also contribute to the much-needed increase in acceptance of the LGBTIQ+ community.

215607.explanation: Public space belongs to everyone and requires careful management. Terraces should be possible but within clear rules.

215608.explanation: By supporting local entrepreneurs in sustainability, the municipality accelerates the transition to a sustainable and future-proof city. The support helps entrepreneurs financially against high energy prices, making them more resilient.

215609.explanation: Repair meetings reduce waste, save raw materials, and strengthen social cohesion. Repairing instead of throwing away is better for the environment and for the wallet. Subsidies for these initiatives are therefore very important.

215611.explanation: Labor migrants must not be sent away if they lose their job or home. The municipality must focus on protection, shelter, and support, not return as a default solution. The responsible employer or agency must provide a replacement job.

215613.explanation: Illegal dumping harms people, animals, and nature. A higher fine can help counter this. Also, waste disposal should be considered more as raw materials. Access to the recycling center must be easier and cheaper. People without transport receive assistance.

215614.explanation: Structural support prevents progress from stalling. Support is needed to sustainably tackle persistent disadvantages in Heerlen and promote equal opportunities. This creates a sense of social justice and thus increases trust in government and connection in the city.

215615.explanation: To promote independent energy cooperatives. Neighborhood batteries make it possible to better use locally generated solar energy and contribute to a stable and fair local energy system.

215616.explanation: A permit is required for large trees, not for small ones. Trees are essential for climate, biodiversity, and livability. They reduce heat stress, provide habitat for birds and insects, purify air, and absorb water. Tree felling must therefore be carefully considered and may not happen without permission.

215617.explanation: Animals do not belong at events where crowding and stress are inevitable, not even for educational purposes. The municipality must always prioritize animal welfare. Animals are not attractions!

215620.explanation: Culture is the foundation of a social society and contributes to well-being, meeting, and a livable city. Cuts to culture particularly affect accessibility for residents and local initiatives. Funding for festivals and cultural events that promote local culture, traditions, and the regional language. No funding for elitist art or multicultural events.

215621.explanation: Replacement is necessary if the sport practiced allows it. Artificial turf causes microplastic pollution and harms the environment and nature. Natural grass is a more sustainable and healthier alternative and also provides better drainage and less pollution.

215622.explanation: Accessible sports facilities in all neighborhoods promote health, social interaction, and prevention. A broad sports offer is important, not just traditional club sports. There should also be focus on multifunctional and more intensive use of existing sports fields.

215623.explanation: Free fitness equipment in public spaces stimulates exercise, is accessible to everyone, and contributes to disease prevention and livability. Existing elements can also be used to create an exercise trail to save raw materials.

215624.explanation: The neutrality of enforcers is shown through their actions, not their appearance. A ban on religious expressions is not necessary for professional functioning and is discriminatory. Population diversity may be visibly expressed.

215625.explanation: Healthcare fraudsters must be tracked down and addressed. The government takes back control, with clear rules, and providers are forced to focus on quality of care instead of profit maximization.

215626.explanation: Fines for public drug or alcohol use do not solve addiction problems. The municipality should focus on care, support, and prevention. Experienced experts should be deployed for care avoiders.

215627.explanation: Mixed neighborhoods are important, but demolition should not be the starting point. Sustainability and reuse of existing buildings must be prioritized over new construction.

215628.explanation: To ensure mixed and livable neighborhoods, sufficient social rental housing must also be added in neighborhoods where it is currently less present. The neighborhood's foundations must be in order with sufficient facilities. The municipality must ensure this in all neighborhoods.

215629.explanation: The municipality must steer towards enough affordable and suitable housing. Without clear frameworks, the market determines what is built, not the needs of residents. Housing is a basic right, not a profit model.

215630.explanation: Trailer park residents also have the right to suitable housing. With sufficient pitches, inequality and exclusion are prevented. Moreover, we also support other alternative and sustainable living forms such as Tiny Houses and the like.

215631.explanation: Animal care should not depend on someone's income. By supporting people with low incomes with necessary veterinary costs, we prevent animal suffering and bigger problems. This comes with the condition of limiting the acquisition of new pets.

215632.explanation: Sanctions in social assistance increase stress and uncertainty and do not help people towards sustainable employment. The municipality should focus on guidance and trust, not punishment. Additionally, it is good to broaden the definition of work: informal care and volunteer work also count as work.

215633.explanation: Support from family or friends should not be penalized. Social assistance should be based on trust and livelihood security, not on suspicion and control.

215634.explanation: Active participation in society should be encouraged, not discouraged. It increases financial independence and provides appreciation and recognition. It can give people the confidence to take positive steps towards the future.

215635.explanation: Older job seekers should also have the chance to find suitable work. With targeted support, the municipality can prevent exclusion and better utilize talent and knowledge.

215636.explanation: Speed bumps are annoying, but an effective way to reduce speed and make residential neighborhoods safer.

215637.explanation: Parking fees are not a means to pay for roads and bike paths (asphalt), but a way to invest in a pleasant city, such as redesigning streets, more trees, good street lighting, and clean air.

215638.explanation: Car sharing reduces car ownership and parking pressure. By designating more spots, the municipality encourages sustainable and efficient mobility. Reduced car ownership not only saves costs but is also better for the environment, as it requires fewer scarce resources.

215639.explanation: Lower speeds in residential areas increase traffic safety for cyclists and pedestrians, reduce nuisance, and contribute to a healthy and livable environment.

215641.explanation: Necessary (basic) aids like walkers must be accessible to everyone who needs them. Financial barriers increase inequality and can lead to bigger care problems later on.

215642.explanation: Informal care is valuable but should never replace professional care. The municipality must support where people want and can help each other, without shifting care away.

215643.explanation: Community care centers enable independent living for the elderly and strengthen social cohesion through meetings and activities. Additionally, they can reduce the feeling of loneliness, which in turn benefits the wellbeing of our residents.

215644.explanation: Access to light youth care should not depend on the parents' income. Income does not tell the whole story about parents' financial situation and could lead to avoiding care. Early support is essential for equal opportunities.

215645.explanation: Bringing together young people and vulnerable elderly creates meaningful encounters and less loneliness. It allows young people to discover their talents. Youngsters should receive personal guidance from care professionals to help seniors as best as possible.

215585.explanation: Lower taxes give residents freedom over their money and stimulate investment and ownership. Higher property taxes (OZB) hinder economic activity and property rights.

215586.explanation: The dog tax is an unfair tax without a clear return. The municipality should not be a profit model over residents' personal choices.

215587.explanation: The market and innovation should determine energy supply. Government support distorts incentives, leads to inefficiency, and limits choices for entrepreneurs and households.

215588.explanation: No legal residence means no right to shelter. Enforcement of laws protects public support and prevents misuse of resources.

215589.explanation: Equal treatment is essential. Priority undermines trust and creates distortion in the housing market.

215590.explanation: Integration is primarily a personal responsibility. Government programs are costly and often inefficient.

215591.explanation: Residents' freedom is central. Restrictions are only justified when there is demonstrable harm to others.

215592.explanation: No unnecessary spending on aesthetics. Efficient maintenance should have priority.

215594.explanation: Visible enforcement increases safety and compliance with rules, without unnecessary bureaucracy.

215595.explanation: Low-threshold contact strengthens residents' trust and involvement in safety.

215596.explanation: Proportional powers are needed to make enforcement effective and maintain order.

215597.explanation: The market should determine redevelopment. Subsidies create inefficiency and can stimulate speculation.

215599.explanation: A basic income undermines work incentives and creates dependency. Sustainable and efficient policy stimulates work, not passive income.

215600.explanation: Free movement and accessibility are crucial for entrepreneurs and residents. The municipality should not dictate technical choices.

215601.explanation: Cars are essential for accessibility and economic activity in the city center.

215602.explanation: Public funds should not favor specific technologies; markets and entrepreneurs can invest themselves.

215603.explanation: Quality and effectiveness are more important than the cheapest provider. Market incentives and result-oriented working provide better care.

215604.explanation: This is not a municipal task.

215605.explanation: Extra oversight protects citizens against misuse of municipal power and increases transparency.

215606.explanation: The municipality must remain neutral. Ideological language politics is not a task of the municipality.

215607.explanation: Entrepreneurial freedom stimulates the local economy and vibrancy. Less bureaucracy promotes initiative.

215608.explanation: Sustainable investments should be the entrepreneurs' own choice. Government support distorts market dynamics and reduces own initiative and responsibility.

215609.explanation: Repair events should arise from citizens and entrepreneurs. Municipal subsidies distort incentives and lead to less sustainable, dependency-prone initiatives.

215611.explanation: By encouraging return, migrant workers remain independent and dependency on municipal support is limited, promoting efficiency and fairness in the system.

215613.explanation: Strict enforcement protects the living environment and property, and strengthens compliance with rules.

215614.explanation: The intentions of the National Programme Heerlen North are good: investments in neighborhoods and opportunities for residents are important. Yet large-scale government programs are often costly and inefficient. More effective solutions arise through market forces, local entrepreneurs, and resident initiatives, which are demand-driven and flexible.

215615.explanation: Technological choices are better left to market parties. Government influence can actually hinder innovation.

215616.explanation: Property rights are sacred. Owners decide about their land; the government may only intervene in case of demonstrable harm to third parties.

215617.explanation: Existing regulations are sufficient. Additional prohibitions restrict freedom and initiative without added effect.

215620.explanation: Culture thrives better without structural subsidies. The market and associations determine supply and quality. We would like to start a pilot with issuing culture vouchers.

215621.explanation: Artificial turf is efficient, sustainable, and cost-effective; replacing it with real grass is unnecessarily expensive.

215622.explanation: Sport is primarily individual responsibility and association initiative. Government spending creates dependency.

215623.explanation: Individual lifestyle choices should not be financed through public funds.

215624.explanation: Borough enforcement officers (Boa's) must remain neutral. Symbolism must not distract from enforcement tasks.

215625.explanation: Healthcare fraud undermines solidarity. Investigation protects taxpayer money, strengthens trust, and ensures help reaches those who really need it.

215626.explanation: Focus on nuisance and safety, not on moralizing criminalization. Citizens retain freedom as long as others are not harmed.

215627.explanation: Social engineering of neighborhoods does not work. Natural distribution via market mechanisms is more effective.

215628.explanation: The municipality should not decide how much social housing is in expensive neighborhoods. Coercion distorts the market and limits property rights, while demand-driven housing construction is more efficient and effective in solving shortages.

215629.explanation: The municipality should not determine what type of housing is built where. Owners and developers know best what is needed, and demand-driven market forces ensure efficient and flexible housing construction without unnecessary government interference.

215630.explanation: Favoring specific groups creates imbalances and limits the freedom of others. Housing policy should be neutral.

215631.explanation: Personal responsibility also applies to pets. Public funds are for real emergencies, not standard costs.

215632.explanation: Incentives are essential. Discounts stimulate participation, prevent dependency, and ensure social assistance remains a temporary safety net.

215633.explanation: Social assistance is a safety net, not a supplement to private income. Discounts on high donations protect support and solidarity.

215634.explanation: This prevents dependency and imbalance. Offsetting additional earnings maintains incentives for work and self-reliance.

215635.explanation: Sustainable employment arises from market needs, not through municipal projects. Government initiatives disrupt incentives and create inefficient jobs.

215636.explanation: Speed bumps limit traffic flow and cause extra costs. Targeted traffic policy is more effective and proportional. Speed bumps only where safety is at risk.

215637.explanation: Revenues must return to roads and bike paths. Using them for other purposes leads to inefficiency and lack of transparency.

215638.explanation: Public funds must not favor any specific mode of mobility. Demand-driven market mechanisms are more efficient.

215639.explanation: Lower speed increases safety in residential areas, protects vulnerable people, and limits nuisance without hindering traffic flow on main roads.

215641.explanation: Public funds should prioritize people who truly cannot afford it. A personal contribution encourages responsibility.

215642.explanation: Subsidiarity: first your own circle, then the government. Family, friends, and neighborhood are the first safety net; the municipality only as a last resort.

215643.explanation: Community building arises better through voluntary initiatives. Large-scale municipal concepts are expensive, bureaucratic, and reduce personal involvement.

215644.explanation: Personal responsibility and cost-awareness are important. The municipality only funds truly necessary care.

215645.explanation: Voluntary initiatives work better than subsidized projects. The municipality should not steer this but facilitate where needed.

215621.explanation: Yes, if it is necessary due to (health) danger.

215622.explanation: Point out possibilities. Especially for clubs and keep sports facilities accessible.

215623.explanation: Some already exist. Indicate where those places are.

215624.explanation: Faith is part of your personality, provided you also respect others’ values.

215625.explanation: Fraud certainly does not belong in care. That money is urgently needed to tackle and solve problems.

215626.explanation: Does not belong in public spaces. It deserves a permanent approach.

215627.explanation: There are currently too many cheap homes. Especially many more middle and upper segment homes must be added. This gives Heerlen more financial strength to move forward together.

215628.explanation: More middle and upper segment homes are needed because Heerlen already has relatively many social housing units.

215629.explanation: The city must rise to a higher level by adding middle and upper housing segments.

215630.explanation: Existing locations must be better used. Empty spots can now be filled.

215631.explanation: Taking an (house)pet comes with joys and responsibilities. If it really cannot be otherwise, consult the veterinarian.

215632.explanation: If you can work, then go to work. Benefits only if it cannot be helped, and then for as short a period as possible.

215633.explanation: If it happens once with good intentions and no bad intent, that is fine. Having a windfall once in a while is allowed.

215634.explanation: Benefits only if it really cannot be helped. Prefer the alternative: work and own income.

215636.explanation: Where necessary and possible: yes. However, decency, also in traffic, is more important.

215637.explanation: Deploy where needed, with rates that must be affordable and attractive. Preferably => free where possible.

215615.explanation: Will this cost more money for residents? Think about cooperating on sustainability.

215616.explanation: If felling is necessary, the procedure for it must be made easier.

215617.explanation: Many children, including those from Greater Heerlen, often do not know where milk and eggs come from!

215620.explanation: Culture is an important link in society for associations where volunteers can be found, among others. Associations cannot do without volunteers.

215621.explanation: In principle, real grass is nicer for sports, but artificial grass is comparatively cheaper for the municipality and clubs because real grass requires more maintenance and after mowing the markings must be restored.

215622.explanation: Because it is healthy and builds social contacts.

215623.explanation: This has happened several times, but unfortunately, it was rarely used.

statements.title: The city centre must remain easily accessible by car.
Heerlen should get its own ombudsman for complaints about the municipality.
statements.moreInfo: An ombudsman or ombudswoman is independent and helps citizens who get stuck with the government. Heerlen does not have its own ombudsman. Residents with complaints about the municipality can go to the National Ombudsman.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: Heerlen should run a trial of a basic income.

titleParts.text: Heerlen moet een proef doen met

titleParts.glossary: With a basic income, people receive a fixed amount per month from the government. They do not have to apply for jobs or perform socially useful work in return for the money.

statements.moreInfo: A basic income is a fixed amount of money that everyone receives from the government each month, regardless of whether you work or have savings. Municipalities may run trials with basic income. A trial can show how recipients experience it and whether, for example, they work more or less. Such a trial is often not a true basic income but a social assistance benefit with less strict rules. People then, for example, do not have to apply for jobs to keep their benefit.

statements.title: The municipality should spend more money on detecting healthcare fraud.
The use of open fireplaces and wood-burning stoves in Heerlen should remain allowed.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging spots for electric cars.
Elderly people who can afford a walker themselves should not receive one from the municipality.
The municipality should spend more money on integrating newcomers into society.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer geld uitgeven aan

titleParts.glossary: Newcomers learn the language and customs of the Netherlands.

statements.title: The municipality should mow public grass more often.
People who use drugs and alcohol in public should not be fined.
Property tax should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.moreInfo: The property tax (OZB) is the annual tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a business property. The amount depends on the WOZ value: the estimated market value of the building or land on January 1 of the previous year. The municipality determines how high the OZB is.

statements.title: The municipality should pay veterinary costs for low-income people.
The municipality should arrange care more often through help from family, friends, or the neighborhood.
The municipality of Heerlen should send gender-neutral letters to residents.
Residents and local entrepreneurs should no longer have to ask the municipality for permission to cut down a tree.
Artificial turf on the sports fields should be replaced with real grass.
Hospitality businesses should be allowed to open small terraces without having to ask the municipality for permission.
The municipality should help migrant workers return to their home country if they lose their job and housing.
Parents should pay themselves for light youth care such as homework tutoring and help with dyslexia.
statements.moreInfo: Youth care is all help for children up to 18 years old. That can be light help such as tutoring, but also more intensive help, such as treatment for psychological problems. Since 2015 municipalities are responsible for youth care. Municipalities often have too little money for this. At the moment Heerlen pays for both light and more intensive care.

statements.title: The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: There should be fewer speed bumps.
There should be more social-rented housing in neighborhoods with mostly expensive owner-occupied homes.
The municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who monitor safety in Heerlen should also be allowed to frisk/search people.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) die in Heerlen controleren op veiligheid moeten ook mogen fouilleren.

statements.moreInfo: BOAs are enforcers who check that the streets remain safe and tidy. Frisking (searching) means an officer feels your clothing or looks in your pockets to see if you have prohibited items, such as weapons. Currently only the police may do this. If this plan goes ahead, BOAs in Heerlen would also get this right.

statements.title: The municipality should fund more free events where residents can repair broken items with experts, such as electrical appliances or bicycles.
The municipality should reduce welfare benefits for people who do not actively look for work.
Vacant offices should be converted into housing with subsidies from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente worden omgebouwd tot woningen.

The municipality should invest in neighborhood batteries to store electricity from solar panels.
